items.title: Evaluate on held-out labels
items.avoid: Reporting ROC-AUC on training predictions or on data used for feature selection without a fresh holdout.
items.prefer: Compute AUC on validation for model selection; report test AUC once after final model choice.
items.why: Leakage inflates AUC and leads to wrong model picks at deployment.
items.example: Split with train_test_split; fit on train; roc_auc_score only on X_val, y_val.

items.title: Feed ranking scores, not hard labels
items.prefer: Use predict_proba positive column, decision_function, or logits from the model head.
items.why: ROC sweeps thresholds — continuous scores are required for a meaningful curve.

items.title: Pair AUC with an operating point
items.avoid: Stopping at a single AUC number without inspecting FPR at required TPR (or vice versa).
items.prefer: Plot ROC, pick threshold from roc_curve where TPR ≥ target with minimal FPR; build confusion matrix at that t.
items.why: AUC selects the scorer; deployment still needs one threshold and concrete error rates.
items.example: After AUC 0.87, choose threshold where TPR=0.90 and report FPR=0.12 from roc_curve.

items.title: Check PR metrics when positives are rare
items.avoid: Trusting ROC-AUC alone on 0.1% positive prevalence without precision context.
items.prefer: Add precision-recall curve or average_precision_score; compare models on both views.
items.why: ROC can mask poor precision on rare events — PR-AUC focuses on the positive class.
items.example: Fraud task: report ROC-AUC and PR-AUC side by side on the same validation fold.
